raw_8_compliance: PASS — no new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml files created. Witness JSON + state .md only. Recommendations target EXISTING grandfathered .sh files.

background.parent_carry_forward_quote: Raw .sh scripts (tool/falsifier_health.sh, tool/atlas_index.sh) have similar `|| python3` patterns that don't fire on macOS. EXCLUDED from this audit per raw#8 hexa-only edit policy. File as separate carry-forward.
background.bug_class: BSD date(1) does NOT support %N / %3N. Emits literal '1776797833N' AND exits 0. Shell `||` fallback never fires; downstream arithmetic silently uses garbage or 0.
background.audit_count_reconciliation: Parent audit reported 'unfixable / out-of-scope = 5'. Concrete .sh sites in nexus repo = 4 (atlas_index.sh:48, atlas_index.sh:76, falsifier_health.sh:153, falsifier_health.sh:155). The 5th of the parent's tally is interpreted as a methodology-line / cross-repo .sh count rounding (no 5th .sh file with the bug exists in nexus). Honesty marker: this cycle scopes ONLY the 2 nexus .sh files it can verify.

five_op_kernel.1_intake: POLICY/DESIGN cycle. NO code changes. Per-file decide A=PORT-to-hexa / B=DEPRECATE-in-place / C=LEAVE-WITH-WARNING (sed-fix the cascade later in a separate impl cycle).

3_axes.axis_per_file_assessment: atlas_index.sh: build-once cache emitter, 131 LOC, well-bounded shell features (grep -nE, sed -nE, while-read, awk, mktemp). Bug-impact: build_ms timing metric only — does NOT drive any guard/comparison; cosmetic-only effect (build_ms might be 0 ms reported on macOS even when build took seconds). falsifier_health.sh: registry runner, 221 LOC, heavier shell features (eval, sha256_16 helper, python3 inline, mktemp, trap). Bug-impact: per-falsifier DUR_MS reported in TSV is wrong on macOS, but DOES NOT alter PASS/FAIL classification (status logic uses ec + grep, not duration). Severity: cosmetic on both files at present (timing reporting only, not control flow). Sibling falsifier_health_parallel.sh is the active default and is bug-FREE (it routes timing through python3 worker).
3_axes.axis_raw_8_spirit_fidelity: raw#8 says 'no NEW .py/.sh/.rs/.toml'. Existing files are grandfathered. raw#8 SPIRIT favors progressive migration to .hexa, so option A (PORT) advances the spirit best, B (DEPRECATE) honors the spirit by reducing surface area, C (LEAVE+FIX) is neutral — keeps a grandfathered file alive but at least kills the silent bypass. Decision must NOT introduce new .sh siblings (e.g. 'falsifier_health_v2.sh') even as transitional shim — that would create-new-sh in spirit-violation. PORT must replace .sh with .hexa (or go via existing .hexa runner), not augment with another .sh.
3_axes.correlation_check: axes are not redundant: per-file assessment focuses on bug-severity + LOC + sibling availability; raw-8 fidelity focuses on policy direction. Estimated correlation 0.45 (< 0.7 threshold) — both genuinely informative.

five_op_kernel.4_tier1_gate: F1+F2+F3+F4 PASS; F5 PASS for honest prioritization. Tier-1 gate CLEAR.

decision_matrix.purpose: Sequential falsifier-registry health checker. Iterates F1..FN from design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl, classifies CLEAN/HIT/ERROR/TAMPERED/SPOOF, appends to state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, emits __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ sentinel. R1 cmd_sha256, R2 anti-spoof, R3-lite registry baseline drift.

bug_sites.impact: T0 ends in literal 'N' on macOS; T1-T0 arithmetic feeds DUR_MS reporting only; downstream classification (CLEAN/HIT/ERROR) uses ec + grep, NOT DUR_MS. Cosmetic — wrong per-falsifier ms in TSV/timeline.
same as :153 — both T0 and T1 end in N, subtraction may be syntactically rejected by bash-arith → DUR_MS=0 silently. Reported in JSONL timeline.

call_sites: tool/health_check_all.sh:50 — FALLBACK only (default is falsifier_health_parallel.sh; line 47)
decision_matrix.active_call_path: INDIRECT (legacy fallback). Active default is falsifier_health_parallel.sh which has NO date %N bug.
decision_matrix.complexity_to_port: HIGH — 221 LOC, eval-based command execution per registry entry, python3 inline for JSONL parsing, trap+mktemp lifecycle, multiple shell-cascading regex (sed -nE / grep -qE), shasum/sha256sum dual-binary fallback. Porting to pure .hexa would require: registry JSONL parser (already in .hexa style), exec() per entry, classification ladder, sentinel emit, timeline JSONL append. Well-defined but long.
decision_matrix.hexa_alternatives: falsifier_health_parallel.sh (293 LOC, also .sh but timing routed via python3 — bug-free). Long-term: a .hexa runner could replace BOTH .sh siblings.
decision_matrix.rationale: falsifier_health_parallel.sh is the active default and is already bug-free. The sequential .sh is a documented fallback for when python3 is missing. Cleanest action: (1) add deprecation header marking it `legacy fallback only`, (2) remove from health_check_all.sh fallback chain (it was added defensively; the parallel sibling itself depends on python3, so the fallback is largely theatrical when python3 is missing — a .hexa runner could be the future fallback), (3) leave the date bug uncorrected since DUR_MS is cosmetic-only and the file is on a deprecation track. PORT (option A) is too expensive for a soon-to-be-retired runner. LEAVE+FIX (option C) wastes effort on a file we want to kill. Honesty: 'soon-to-be-retired' is contingent on a future .hexa-native falsifier-runner cycle being scheduled — flagged in carry-forward.

decision_matrix.purpose: Build state/atlas_index.tsv from atlas.n6 + atlas.append.*.n6 (id → line_no, type, shard). Accelerates atlas-ingest's _check_dup_in_atlas (replaces O(file_size) grep per insert). Build-once cache. Three modes: build / --lookup <id> / --stats / --refresh. Self-host avoidance (bash + grep, no hexa dep).

bug_sites.impact: BSD `date +%s%N` emits e.g. `1777246909N`. `cut -c1-13` selects 13 chars from the LEFT — on macOS this returns `1777246909N` (unix-seconds + literal N), which is then fed to bash arithmetic. `||` never fires (date exit 0). Result: build_ms reported as garbage / arithmetic error → 0. COSMETIC: build_ms is reported in sentinel and stdout, but does not gate any control flow.
same as :48 — start and end both garbage-N, subtraction yields 0 or arithmetic error on macOS. build_ms displayed as 0 ms even when actual elapsed time is several seconds.

call_sites: tool/atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh:74 — emits 'run: bash tool/atlas_index.sh' as fix-suggestion when state/atlas_index.tsv missing (advisory only, not direct invocation)
decision_matrix.active_call_path: DIRECT but on-demand. No cron, no plist, no daemon — invoked manually or via `nexus index`. Output state/atlas_index.tsv is consumed by atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh + (potentially) atlas-ingest dedup.
decision_matrix.complexity_to_port: LOW–MEDIUM — 131 LOC, 3 well-bounded modes (build / lookup / stats), shell features used: grep -nE, sed -nE, awk -F'\t', wc -c, while-read, sort | uniq -c. All trivially expressible in .hexa via exec() with grep/sed/awk pipelines, OR pure-hexa string manipulation since atlas.n6 line format is `@<TYPE> <id> = ...` (regex-free split feasible). The TSV writer is a few printf calls. No eval, no python3, no shasum cascade. Estimated port: ~150–200 LOC of .hexa.
decision_matrix.hexa_alternatives: None today. The script self-describes as 'self-host 회피 (bash + grep, no hexa dep)' — explicit early-stage choice that is now stale given raw#8 direction.
decision_matrix.rationale: Best raw#8-spirit alignment among all options for this file: (1) low-medium port complexity (no shell-only features that resist hexa expression), (2) the file is direct-invoked (not behind a sibling), (3) the cache-builder semantics suit .hexa well (atlas-ingest itself is .hexa, so the index-builder living in .hexa makes the dependency graph flat instead of hexa→sh→atlas), (4) a .hexa replacement can co-exist via cli/run.hexa replacing line 5728 to `print(atlas_index_run(...))` directly without an exec hop. DEPRECATE (B) is wrong — there's no replacement; killing the file kills the cache. LEAVE+FIX (C) is acceptable but does not advance raw#8 spirit; the bug is cosmetic so 'fix' is low-value while a port pays a permanent dividend.

honesty_markers: audit_5_count_vs_4_sites: parent audit reported 5 unfixable .sh sites; this cycle finds only 4 concrete sites in 2 nexus files. Documented openly; no synthesis to make 5 fit.

deprecation_assumes_future_followup: B for falsifier_health.sh is correct ONLY if a .hexa-native falsifier runner is eventually scheduled; otherwise we are leaving a bug-prone file alive indefinitely. Carry-forward 'falsifier-health-hexa-runner-followup' makes this explicit.

cosmetic_severity_caveat: both bug sites are cosmetic-only (timing-report only, not control-flow). If a future caller starts using DUR_MS / build_ms as a guard, severity flips to functional — this caveat is recorded so the next reader knows to re-evaluate.

no_call_path_unknown: every active invocation of both files was traced to a concrete location (cli/run.hexa, health_check_all.sh, atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh). No 'UNKNOWN' bucket needed for this cycle.
